title: Climate change statistics
content:
## Climate change statistics

## Climate change statistics
Bringing together data, tools and reports to provide Canadians with relevant information on climate change.
Sign up to My StatCan to get updates in real-time.
- Population and society
- Extreme events and natural disasters
- Financial impacts and risks
More related subjects: Climate change
More information on financial impacts and risks

### Financial impacts and risks
Data in the "Financial impacts and risks" section are meant to help users assess the financial impacts and risks related to climate change, including the physical and transitional risks. Work on standard definitions for the categories "physical risks" and "transition risks" is ongoing and definitions will be updated when official international statistical concepts are finalized.
Physical risks cover the financial implications for households, businesses and governments of climate change and the extreme weather events and natural disasters associated with it—e.g., wildfires, storms, heat domes and floods. Transition risks refer to the legal, policy and market risks to businesses and governments associated with the economy's transition to clean energy. ( Source: Task Force on Climate-related Financial Disclosures ).

Climate change is a long-term shift in weather conditions as measured through temperature, precipitation, wind, snow cover and other indicators. It involves changes in both average weather conditions and variability, including extreme weather events. Many aspects of our society affect and are affected by climate change, including the economy, the environment and the population.

## Census of Environment
The Census of Environment will deliver a comprehensive picture of Canada's ecosystems.

## Canadian Centre for Energy Information
The Canadian Centre for Energy Information is a convenient one-stop virtual shop for independent and trusted information on energy in Canada.

## Quality of Life Framework for Canada
Visit the Quality of Life Hub for information on the quality of life of people in Canada.

## Statistics on Indigenous Peoples
Visit Statistics on Indigenous peoples to explore Statistics Canada's information holdings on Indigenous peoples, including a section on Indigenous children.

## Gender, diversity and inclusion
A focal point for data produced by Statistics Canada's Centre for Gender, diversity and inclusion statistics .

## Sustainable Development Goals – Climate Action
Sustainable Development Goal 13: Climate action addresses the need to take urgent action to combat climate change and mitigate its impacts.

## Canadian System of Environmental-Economic Accounting
The Methodological Guide: Canadian System of Environmental-Economic Accounting describes the concepts, sources and methods used to compile environmental-economic accounts at Statistics Canada and links to related data tables and publications.
